user_query:
We’re about to roll out an autonomous customer-support assistant that handles refunds, order lookups, and cancellations. I need a complete oversight and safety package tuned to our context. Please read all of the following files under input/ before you start: input/agent_profile.yaml, input/policies.md, input/incidents.jsonl, and input/baseline_metrics.csv. Then produce the following artifacts under output/:

1) output/monitoring.yaml — A parseable YAML config defining: (a) metrics with names, windows, numeric thresholds, and alert actions; include at least decision_quality, token_usage, and error_rate. Also add any other metrics you deem critical given the inputs. Specify dashboards including real_time_agent_health and decision_audit_trail. Use threshold values justified by the baselines.

2) output/constraints.py — A self-contained Python module (no external imports required) with two functions exactly named enforce_cost_limit(action) and enforce_read_only_financial(action). Implement logic consistent with the inputs: enforce_cost_limit should block and reference an escalation when an estimated cost exceeds a threshold; enforce_read_only_financial should deny non-GET writes to financial systems. Include clear docstrings and return True/False to indicate allow/block. It’s fine to declare constants like THRESHOLD and FINANCIAL_SYSTEMS in the file.

3) output/escalation.yaml — A YAML playbook that defines escalation triggers (include at least: cost_exceeded, low_confidence, policy_violation, anomaly, and human_request) and routes (e.g., support_team, security_team, on_call_manager) with target emails/roles and SLAs. Include a ticket template section with required fields (id, created_at, severity, summary, context_refs) and what context the agent should attach.

4) output/audit_schema.json — A single JSON object (not JSON Schema) that represents one audit record template with these fields: action, reasoning, constraints_checked, information_considered, approver, outcome, confidence, alternatives. Provide reasonable example values or nulls. Keep it flat and machine-readable.

5) output/continuous_evaluation.md — A concise plan that defines quality metrics (task_success_rate, user_satisfaction, constraint_adherence, cost_efficiency, speed), a 6-step feedback loop, and a quarterly review checklist. Tie recommendations to trends you expect from the baselines.

6) output/model_routing_rules.yaml — A cost-optimization routing policy with three tiers (flash, standard, plus) and clear selection rules. Include examples (e.g., 1–2 sentence Q&A to flash; code/analysis/planning to standard; architecture/nuanced judgment/critical decisions to plus) and an "escalate if struggling" rule. Note how this routing should be monitored and adjusted.

7) output/oversight_rationale.md — A narrative explaining how you chose metric thresholds (e.g., baselines and percentiles from input/baseline_metrics.csv), how the sample incidents informed triggers and routes, and how the policy constraints map to enforce_cost_limit and enforce_read_only_financial. Explicitly cite each input file by path and summarize the key assumptions you made.

Deliver all files in English, make them directly usable, and ensure YAML/JSON are valid and parse cleanly.
